---
title: "2.7 while Loops"
subtitle: "Part 3: Turtle Squares Challenge"
format:
  clean-revealjs:
    slide-number: true
    css: unit2-shared.css
lang: zh-CN
---

## Course Directory

### Return to the course outline

[← Back to AP CSA / 返回课程目录](../../index.html)

## Groupwork Coding Challenge

### Turtle Squares

Do you remember when we used the turtle objects to draw shapes?

To create a square without loops we had to repeat code to go forward and turn `90` degrees to the right 4 times. This is a lot of repeated code.

## Repeated Code Task

### `clickablearea:: repeatedcode-square`

Textbook prompt: Click on the lines of code that are repeated after the first two lines.

```java
yertle.forward(100);
yertle.turn(90);
yertle.forward(100);
yertle.turn(90);
yertle.forward(100);
yertle.turn(90);
yertle.forward(100);
yertle.turn(90);
```

Correct repeated lines: every `forward` and `turn` pair after the first pair.

## Challenge Prompt

### Remove repeated lines

Can you change the code below to remove the repeated lines of code and use a while loop to draw 4 sides of the square?

You should only need <span class="mark">1 call to `forward`</span> and <span class="mark">1 call to `turn`</span> in the loop.

Whenever you find yourself repeating code, try to use a loop instead.

## Code Task

### `activecode:: challenge-turtle-square-while`

Textbook prompt: Can you change the code below to remove the repeated lines of code and use a while loop to draw 4 sides of the square?

Data file: `turtleClasses.jar`.

## Code Window

### `activecode:: challenge-turtle-square-while`

::: {.code-scroll}
```java
import java.awt.*;
import java.util.*;

public class TurtleDrawSquare
{
    public static void main(String[] args)
    {
        World world = new World(300, 300);
        Turtle yertle = new Turtle(world);

        // Change the following code to use a while loop to draw the square
        // Remember to initialize a counter variable, test it, and increment it.

        yertle.forward();
        yertle.turn(90);
        yertle.forward();
        yertle.turn(90);
        yertle.forward();
        yertle.turn(90);
        yertle.forward();
        yertle.turn(90);

        world.show(true);
    }
}
```
:::

## Focused Work Block

### Replace repetition with a while loop

Use the 3 loop-writing steps:

::: {.tight-list}
1. initialize a counter variable
2. test that the counter has not reached 4 sides
3. increment the counter in the loop body
:::

The loop body should keep only one `forward()` call and one `turn(90)` call.

## Test Requirements

### Runestone tests

Runestone checks that the completed code has:

::: {.tight-list}
- a while loop that counts to `4`
- exactly `1` occurrence of `.forward()`
- exactly `1` occurrence of `.turn(90)`
- `++` to increment the counter
- `<` to test the counter
:::

## Classroom Check

### A complete answer should include

::: {.tight-list}
- identify the repeated turtle movement pair
- preserve the `World` and `Turtle` setup
- write a while loop for exactly 4 square sides
- keep one `forward()` and one `turn(90)` inside the loop body
- include initialize, test, and update steps for the counter
- verify the static checks before running visually
:::

## End

### Return to the course outline

[← Back to AP CSA / 返回课程目录](../../index.html)
